Velvet Room
Cocktails Experience Journal Reserve
Cocktail Bar • Late Nights

Drinks. Mood. Precision.

Velvet Room is built around cocktails and atmosphere first. The structure is magazine-like and lounge-led, completely different from the restaurant layout while keeping a premium, professional look.

Tonight's Mood

Dark, polished, and drinks-forward.

The bar page is structured like a nightlife editorial, not a restaurant menu. It feels focused on cocktails, lounge culture, and late-night service.

Opening Hours6 PM — 2 AM
Music DirectionDeep house / lounge
ServiceBooth reservations & bottle service
House Focus

Signature cocktails, curated spirits, and a room designed for longer nights.

Everything is centered around bar culture — the pacing, lighting, and service are designed to support the cocktail experience.

Signature Mixes Reserve Booths Late Lounge

A split cocktail menu instead of food-style cards.

The bar page uses dual menu columns and a stronger drinks-first structure, which keeps it visually separate from the restaurant design.

Signature Cocktails

Espresso Martini$13

Vodka, espresso, coffee liqueur, and a polished foam finish with bold late-night depth.

Berry Velvet$12

Dark berry infusion, citrus lift, and a richer visual profile designed for evening service.

Golden Citrus$11

Bright citrus structure with premium spirits and a cleaner, more refreshing finish.

Classic Pours

Old Fashioned$14

Bourbon, bitters, orange oils, and a slow-stirred profile with timeless bar character.

Midnight Blue$12

A cool-toned signature pour with citrus notes, layered spirits, and a dramatic finish.

House Tasting Flight$18

A curated mini-selection of signature pours for guests who want variety in one order.

The bar experience should feel more intimate than a club and more focused than a restaurant.

This middle section shifts into a feature-story structure, built around mood, lounge identity, and precision service.

Refined Late-Night Energy

Soft shadows, premium materials, and a darker visual system help the bar feel elevated without becoming a nightclub.

Drinks-Led Service

The guest journey is centered around cocktails, tasting recommendations, and a smoother lounge-style pace.

Reserved Seating & Booths

Perfect for date nights, private corners, and groups who want a more polished social atmosphere.

Visual Identity

The overall composition is intentionally sleek and editorial, so the page feels like a serious hospitality brand.

A journal-style closing section instead of a gallery wall.

The bar finishes with a feature story and guest quote, which keeps the layout totally different from both the restaurant and nightclub.

Built for slower starts and longer nights.

Velvet Room is about the ritual of ordering well, staying longer, and letting the atmosphere do the rest.

Guest Review
“It feels like a real cocktail house — polished, intimate, and far more distinctive than a standard bar website.”

That is the exact impression this layout is built to create.
